description: Self-replicating supply-chain worm signatures (Shai-Hulud, Sha1-Hulud, GlassWorm). Strings or path patterns that survive across worm-republished packages and are very rare in legitimate code.

entries.description: Literal worm name observed in payload strings, repo names, and commit messages of the npm worm waves of Sep 2025 (Shai-Hulud) and Nov 2025 (Sha1-Hulud).
entries.source: Unit 42, Sysdig, Datadog, Microsoft Threat Intel

entries.description: Shai-Hulud propagation pattern: republished packages ship a single 'bundle.js' that is invoked by a postinstall hook. The combination of this filename + postinstall is rare in legitimate packages.
webhook.site is the primary exfil endpoint family used by Shai-Hulud variants. Hard-coded URLs of this shape in package source are almost always exfil-callback URLs.
Worm credential-enumeration shape: read CI/cloud secrets from process.env then POST them. Loose proximity to keep regex bounded; combine with content-only files to avoid FPs in test code.
Self-propagating VS Code / Open VSX extension worm (Oct 2025). Literal string is a strong signal; almost always either researcher commentary or extracted sample.
Shai-Hulud 2.0 exfil pattern: enumerate victim's repo list and create attacker-controlled repos containing stolen secrets. Loose pattern; combine with WORM-SH1/SH2 to escalate.
